behavior_summary: Controls the NPC Bane's interactive gameplay behavior: dialog/look text, area-trigger reactions, theft response escalation, scheduled positioning, 'use' interactions, and a cast action that marks Bane as enraged and delegates actual spell/combat work to a combat spell handler.
BANE::cast (Func_80, Event 11) sets a persistent enraged state (baneMad = 3) then immediately spawns SPELCMBT::084D to perform the combat/spell effect, suspending until that spawned action returns.

AvatarStoleSomething (Event 13) implements a progressive theft-response: warns the avatar, may place the avatar in stasis, set Bane's combat target, increment baneWarned, and escalate to attack if warnings accumulate.

enterFastArea (Event F) runs spatial/scene transition logic when an avatar enters Bane's trigger area: iterates area items and may destroy or reposition them, teleport or move Bane depending on scenario flags (acolyteKilled, goToInnerSanctum, sponsorTest, keyOfTheAcolyte, inDaemonScene), and spawn movement/behavior scripts.
schedule (Event 8) periodically repositions or changes Bane's frame/state according to major scenario flags and may teleport or spawn MOVE/METHOD scripts when not in special scenes.

use (Event 1) routes player interactions into different sequences (BANE2.* or DAEMSCEN.*) depending on flags (baneMad, baneJoined, keyOfTheAcolyte, acolyteKilled) and ends by spawning a standard METHOD::1090 animation/behavior.
notable_control_flow: Most interactive handlers use early-exit guards when Npc::isDead is true — many action paths are skipped if Bane is dead.
Cast immediately writes baneMad=3 then spawns an external combat script (SPELCMBT::084D) and suspends until it returns.

AvatarStoleSomething uses branching on global flags (baneJoined, sponsorTest, keyOfTheAcolyte) to either ignore, warn, or punish the thief; it increments baneWarned to escalate behavior over repeated thefts.

Area-enter (enterFastArea) loops over items found in the trigger region and may call Item::destroy or FREE::2A56, then conditionally teleports or spawns movement methods for Bane based on combined scenario flags.
Schedule and use handlers frequently spawn engine METHODS and MOVE scripts rather than performing long inline sequences, delegating animation/motion to reusable methods.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of acolyteKilled (multi-bit checks) and how its different values map to the distinct teleport/movement branches.
Precise texts and player-facing dialog choices assembled by Func_782 (dialog builder) beyond the inferred examples — strings are inferred but not enumerated here.
Whether baneMad values other than 3 are meaningful elsewhere (cast forcibly sets to 3; other writes/reads may interpret other values).
Full behavior of some spawned internal scripts (BANE2::0080, BANE2::1162, BANE2::170E, DAEMSCEN::*): their concrete game effects are inferred but not disassembled here.

main_function.name: U8P_BANE::cast (Func_80, Event 11)
main_function.purpose: When Bane performs a cast action, mark Bane as enraged (baneMad=3) and delegate the actual combat/spell effect to the combat-spell handler SPELCMBT::084D; suspend until the spawned spell completes.
entry_conditions: Triggered by Event 11 (cast) on the Bane NPC.
Operates on the NPC instance (implicit this) and expects a pid/process and an additional forwarded argument ([BP+0Ah]) representing the spell target or parameter.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::cast
function_map.role: Handle Bane's cast action: set enraged state and spawn SPELCMBT::084D to perform the spell/combat effect.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Respond to avatar theft in Bane's area: warn, increment baneWarned, place avatar in stasis, set combat target, and spawn follow-up behavior or punishment.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::look
function_map.role: Return or bark a textual description of Bane, choosing variants based on baneName, baneJob and Npc::isDead.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduling: reposition Bane, change frames, and spawn movement/teleport methods depending on scenario flags.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered when an avatar enters Bane's fast-area: iterate items, destroy or adjust them, and teleport or move Bane based on flags (scene transitions).

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup when an actor leaves Bane's trigger area (calls FREE::2A56 / cleanup routine).

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'use' interactions with Bane, spawning appropriate BANE2 or DAEMSCEN scripts or METHOD::1090 depending on state flags.

function_map.name: U8P_BANE::0782
function_map.role: Dialog/question builder: assemble conversation options based on baneHasAsked, baneName and baneJob to present choices to the player.

internal_calls.purpose: After handling theft/warnings, spawn or schedule enterFastArea to process area items or continue behavior flow.
Behaviorally related: use may trigger repositioning/scene scripts; schedule governs periodic repositioning (relationship inferred).
Dialog builder queries look/state to decide which strings/options to expose (inferred relation rather than explicit call).

external_calls.purpose: Perform the actual combat/spell effect; cast delegates the mechanics to the spell-combat handler and waits for its result.
Cleanup/destroy area items or remove objects found when handling theft/area triggers.
Place the avatar into stasis (prevent movement/action) during warning/punishment sequences.
Set Bane's combat/AI target to the avatar when escalating to attack.
Remove items present in Bane's trigger region as part of scene cleanup/transition.
Compute facing/direction for direction-dependent METHOD spawn (animation/movement).
Teleport Bane to predetermined coordinates for scene transitions or repositioning.
Spawn movement behavior when teleport is not used; move Bane toward a location instead of instant reposition.
Spawn secondary Bane behavior/dialog sequences depending on flags (alternate behavior when Bane has joined or other quest state).
Enter or coordinate special daemon-scene sequences when relevant flags indicate such a transition.
Display the appropriate look/description string to the viewing process.
Play a standard METHOD sequence (animation/behavior) following use interactions.

reads.name: baneJoined
reads.likely_effect: Changes responses and which BANE2 behaviors are spawned (treat Bane as ally/member vs. hostile NPC).

reads.name: sponsorTest
reads.likely_effect: Gates movement/teleport or behavior in area-enter logic and theft handling (quest gating).

reads.name: keyOfTheAcolyte
reads.likely_effect: Alters dialog and area/scene transitions when the player holds this key item.

reads.name: baneWarned
reads.likely_effect: Counter used by AvatarStoleSomething to escalate from warnings to attack; read to choose which bark/behavior to use.

reads.name: baneName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Bane reveals a proper name string in look/dialog logic.

reads.name: baneJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Bane reveals his job/title (e.g., 'Sorcerer') in look/dialog logic.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Major scene flag that changes teleport/position branches, movement and aggression; treated as multi-bit state with several branch outcomes.

reads.name: goToInnerSanctum
reads.likely_effect: Combined with acolyteKilled to decide inner-sanctum teleport/movement behavior.

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Prevents or alters normal movement/spawn behavior when Bane or the game is in a special daemon scene.

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: When set, Bane may set the avatar as its target/master or modify target-assignment behavior on area entry.

reads.name: baneHasAsked
reads.likely_effect: Used by the dialog builder to avoid offering previously asked questions and to tailor the conversation options.

writes.name: baneMad
writes.likely_effect: Set to 3 by BANE::cast to mark Bane as enraged; used to modify later interactions (behavioral gating in use(), others).

writes.name: baneWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented/updated by AvatarStoleSomething to track number of warnings given to the thief and drive escalation.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Controls the NPC Bane's interactive gameplay behavior: dialog/look text, area-trigger reactions, theft response escalation, scheduled positioning, 'use' interactions, and a cast action that marks Bane as enraged and delegates actual spell/combat work to a combat spell handler.
